title: Checking if creation time is between 20:00 & 08:00

posts.author: irenas (irena shafir Erez)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey

i’m trying to create a workflow / automation that will notify someone if an item is created between 20:00 & 08:00.

i didnt see anyway to do this without a third party app. would love to get some ideas on how it can be done

posts.author: JCorrell (Jim - The Monday Man)
posts.cookedHtml:
@irenas

There is currently no way to do this in base monday. Desiree has a great solution using monday AI. See below. If there is not an app that does that (and I don’t think there is), you will need to use the API in conjunction with something like Make.com or Zappier to check the item’s creation time and send a notification when an item is created.

Jim - The Monday Man
Get Custom Apps, Integrations & Automations for monday

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml:
You can use an automation and the ‘hour’ column to set that column to the hour it was created. Then use AI to categorize by ‘timeframe’ created?

image2198×952 178 KB

image1914×942 29.1 KB

Desiree - www.thecleverclovers.com

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks Jim. Depending on the number of items created, might not be the best solution but it’s definitely an option.

posts.author: Dharmendra_TF (Dharmendra Mishra)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi irena,

We can implement this for you via a custom Monday.com app.
The app will detect when an item is created between 20:00 and 08:00 and automatically send notifications to the relevant person or team — no third-party tool required.

If you’d like, we can share a quick outline of how it will work along with an estimated timeline.

Best regards,
